subject: red pandas
one_liner: Point-of-sale and merchant payments for an animal with no money, no thumbs, and nothing to sell.
brand_voice_notes: Deadpan monetary-authority gravitas. Tender presents itself as a central bank crossed with a payments processor, issuing legal tender and merchant accounts to red pandas with total sincerity. Treats bamboo as a settlement currency, a tree hollow as a deposit account, and the ring pattern on a tail as a payment credential. Never winks. Talks about float, settlement windows, and interchange the way a real processor does, then applies all of it to a solitary forager who will never transact. The figures are minted, not marketed.

hero.h1: A merchant account for the canopy.
hero.sub: Tender issues every red panda a payment terminal, a settlement currency, and same-day deposits to the nearest hollow. Tail-print authorization. No card to lose in a river. Your balance has read zero for six weeks, and it has read zero on time.

features.title: Bark-mounted terminal
features.blurb: We bolt a contactless reader to the trunk of your home tree. Customers brush past, the canopy charges, you keep foraging. Most pandas never touch the hardware. That is the design, not a defect.

features.title: Tail-print authorization
features.blurb: Every payment is signed by the ring pattern on your tail. No PIN, no password, no card you would lose crossing a stream. If the rings match, the money moves. The rings always match. There is no one else up here.

features.title: Settlement to your hollow
features.blurb: Funds clear to your registered tree hollow before sundown, denominated in bamboo at a rate we set nightly and decline to explain. We deduct a modest fee for canopy infrastructure and a slightly larger one we will cover in onboarding.

pricing_tiers.name: Cub
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the recently weaned, monetizing their first branch.

pricing_tiers.name: Forager
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the panda doing real volume across more than one tree.
perks: Up to four terminals across three trees
A floating bamboo rate, disclosed quarterly

pricing_tiers.name: Apex Canopy
pricing_tiers.tagline: For the dominant panda in the range. You know which one you are.
perks: Priority placement on the Den Registry
We stop asking what you are selling

testimonials.quote: I have no product, no customers, and no hands. Tender has settled zero bamboo into my hollow on time, every sundown, for six weeks. I cannot fault the consistency.
testimonials.name: Pemba
testimonials.title: Solitary Forager, Eastern Himalayan Range

testimonials.quote: The terminal is bolted to my favorite napping branch. I have not moved it. I will not move it. It is now part of the branch and, in a sense, part of me.
testimonials.name: Mei-Lin
testimonials.title: Owner-Operator, Three-Tree Holding

testimonials.quote: They told me tail-print authorization could not be forged. Then they reversed a payment I never made. I asked no further questions and renewed at the higher tier.
testimonials.name: Tashi
testimonials.title: Apex Canopy Member, Bamboo Sector Seven

demo_screen_concept: A merchant point-of-sale console for a single red panda's bamboo stand, titled 'Canopy Register'. A tappable grid of bamboo SKUs (Tender Shoots, Day-Old Leaves, Premium Heartwood, Whole Culm, Second-Growth Sprigs) each with a denomination in stalks; tapping an item adds it to a running cart on the right with a live line-item list and a minted subtotal in tabular figures. A large 'Charge tail' button opens a tail-print authorization modal showing a slowly drawing guilloche tail-ring pattern that 'verifies' after a beat and drops the charge into a live settlement feed below, where every cleared transaction reads 0 stalks. A header strip shows the merchant's hollow balance pinned at '0 stalks — settled, on time', a settlement-window countdown to sundown, and tabs switching between Register, Settlements, and the Hollow deposit account. All data is mocked client-side.

design_direction.style: Art-deco intaglio banknote, warm monetary-authority mode. The page is laid out like legal tender issued by a central bank: a warm banknote-ivory ground carries fine guilloche linework (engraved rosettes, wave-line borders, and a faint security-lathe pattern rendered in SVG), intaglio hairline frames around every panel, and oversized engraved denomination numerals. One deep intaglio petrol-teal carries the brand, the lathe linework, frame rules, and all structural ink; one rationed minted-copper is reserved strictly for the single primary CTA, the live 'charge' affordance, and the verified-payment seal, and appears almost nowhere else. Cinzel sets the wordmark, headlines, and big denomination figures in monumental engraved caps, like the face of a banknote; Spectral carries calm old-style body copy. Every figure that pretends to be money (balances, settlement windows, stalk prices, line-item totals) is set in Spectral with tabular old-style figures so it reads like a minted ledger entry rather than a cold console monospace. Ornament is structural, not decorative: corner rosettes anchor panels, a guilloche medallion frames the engraved red-panda portrait, and a wave-line rule separates every section. No gradients beyond a faint paper vignette, no glassmorphism, no neon, no drop-shadow marketing cards, no monospace HUD telemetry, no dark mode. Reads like a sober national mint that has been pointed, with complete seriousness, at a solitary tree-dwelling animal that has never once needed to be paid.
design_direction.archetype: Fintech payments / point-of-sale landing rendered as engraved legal tender: a banknote hero with a guilloche-framed engraved red-panda portrait medallion, an oversized Cinzel wordmark and denomination numerals, three feature panels divided by wave-line intaglio rules, three pull-quote testimonials in minted oval medallions, a three-tier pricing rack styled as note denominations, a rationed minted-copper single CTA, and a demo handoff to the Canopy Register point-of-sale console.
design_direction.motion_vocab: Engraved and minted, never flashy: intaglio hairline frames draw in on scroll like a stroke of the burin, the guilloche portrait medallion rotates its lathe pattern very slowly, the copper CTA presses inward like a struck stamp, ledger rows settle on a soft ease, and the tail-print pattern draws stroke-by-stroke before sealing. Micro-interactions 150-280ms, ease-out entering, exits faster; all motion gated behind prefers-reduced-motion.
design_direction.hero_composition_notes: Banknote hero on the ivory ground framed by an intaglio wave-line border with corner rosettes. Left column carries the engraved Cinzel 'TENDER' wordmark, a small 'Issued for the canopy' kicker, the deadpan h1, sub, the minted-copper 'Open a merchant account' CTA, and a quiet 'open the demo' link. Right column is a guilloche medallion holding the engraved red-panda portrait, with a large engraved denomination numeral and a faint security-lathe rosette behind it, so the whole fold reads like the face of a banknote. A wave-line rule closes the hero.

images.rationale: The whole concept is engraved legal tender, so an engraved red-panda banknote portrait genuinely earns the hero slot and anchors the central-bank conceit. The demo is a point-of-sale console (document/dashboard-shaped, not photo-shaped), so no demo subject assets were generated. The remaining budget went to three engraved red-panda portraits for the named testimonials, the always-applicable fallback, all rendered as intaglio currency engravings so they sit natively inside minted-medallion frames and harmonize with the petrol-teal and minted-copper palette. Budget: 4 of 4 used, all intaglio engravings; all four passed vision judging on the first attempt.

items.prompt: Intaglio line-engraving portrait of a single red panda in banknote-engraving style, three-quarter view, composed official expression, deep petrol-teal ink line-work (#1C4A47) on warm banknote-ivory (#F3E9D2) with minted-copper (#B0742B) fine-line accents and a faint guilloche security-lathe behind; sober national-mint currency aesthetic; no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI.
items.judge: pass — correct subject, true intaglio cross-hatched engraving, faint guilloche rosette behind, on-palette teal ink on ivory, no text artifacts, reads as the face of a banknote

items.prompt: Intaglio line-engraving head-and-shoulders portrait of a red panda, banknote style, three-quarter looking slightly off-camera, calm solitary expression, petrol-teal ink (#1C4A47) on banknote-ivory (#F3E9D2), minted-copper (#B0742B) fine accents, faint guilloche lathe behind; no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI.
items.judge: pass — distinct three-quarter pose, engraved square intaglio frame, clean, on-palette

items.prompt: Intaglio line-engraving head-and-shoulders portrait of a red panda, banknote style, front-facing, dignified faintly stern official expression, petrol-teal ink (#1C4A47) on banknote-ivory (#F3E9D2), minted-copper (#B0742B) fine accents, faint guilloche lathe behind; no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI.
items.judge: pass — direct front gaze ideal for a deadpan testimonial, oval guilloche behind, no artifacts

items.prompt: Intaglio line-engraving head-and-shoulders portrait of a red panda, banknote style, near-profile turned aside, authoritative gaze into the middle distance, petrol-teal ink (#1C4A47) on banknote-ivory (#F3E9D2), minted-copper (#B0742B) fine accents, faint guilloche lathe behind; no text/letters/numbers/logos/watermarks/UI.
items.judge: pass — authoritative near-profile in an ornate circular guilloche medallion, ringed tail visible, strongly on-brief
